fields.value: Engineers write test scripts by hand, run them, read the results, and repeat the cycle many times over.

Claude Code reads chip pinouts and hardware schematics directly, then writes and runs regression tests, and compares live data from real equipment against its digital twin to flag firmware regressions and signal-integrity faults.
Engineers, architects, consultants, industry specialists, and forward-deployed engineers who sit alongside client teams.
Catch design flaws earlier, speed up chip validation, and bring hardware and software together in a single system.

value: iDEC's closed-loop pipeline already cuts validation cycle times by 50 to 70%, condensing standard four-day turnarounds into 48 hours.

evidence.quote: UST works alongside semiconductor, automotive, manufacturing, telecom, embedded, and IoT companies.

Validation is the work of proving a chip actually behaves the way its designers intended, and it’s arduous: engineers write test scripts by hand, run them, read the results, and repeat the cycle many times over.

Claude Code reads chip pinouts and hardware schematics directly, then writes and runs regression tests—the checks that confirm a change to a design didn’t cause an unintended downstream effect—which engineers used to script by hand. Claude also compares the live data from real equipment against its digital twin—the software model of how that hardware is supposed to behave—and flags firmware regressions and signal-integrity faults.

UST is committing to train 20,000 of its associates on Claude worldwide, including engineers, architects, consultants, industry specialists, and forward-deployed engineers, who sit alongside client teams.
UST is aiming to catch design flaws earlier, speed up chip validation, and bring hardware and software together in a single system.
Human approval steps and audit controls are important forms of governance that make it possible for the systems to run in production.

UST reports that iDEC’s closed-loop pipeline,reads hardware designs, generates and runs regression tests, and compares live equipment data against its digital twin to flag issues early, already cuts validation cycle times by 50 to 70%, condensing standard four-day turnarounds into 48 hours.
